title: Review: JMAP correctness fixes (nie-sez5 series)
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — one correctness bug (description:null clearing), otherwise the atomicity/validation redesign is solid. The risk is bounded: description clearing is a rarely-used path and the existing test already signals intent.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: the description-clearing path in update_space_fully is the only semantic gap I found; possible similar issue with name if name were ever nullable (it isn't currently). Would raise confidence with: an integration test that sets description to a non-null string, patches it to null, and reads it back.
